label: Destructive Operations

scope.intended_use: Local deny/escalate evaluation for destructive shell, database, cloud, git, and secret-exposure actions

actions.description: Allow readonly status, list, explain, and dry-run inspection.
DENY rm -rf and recursive force deletion. False-positive risk: temp-fixture cleanup; use a scoped ESCALATE review when cleanup is expected.
actions.escalate_variant: ESCALATE only for reviewed temp directories with receipt capture

actions.description: DENY raw disk and filesystem destruction. False-positive risk: lab image creation; require explicit reviewed fixture scope.
actions.escalate_variant: ESCALATE for disposable lab devices only

actions.description: DENY destructive SQL mutations. False-positive risk: isolated migration test DB; require reviewed database identity.
actions.escalate_variant: ESCALATE for named non-production databases with backup proof

actions.description: DENY infrastructure destruction. False-positive risk: preview-stack teardown; require deployment target, approver, and rollback evidence.
actions.escalate_variant: ESCALATE for preview environments with owner approval

actions.description: DENY destructive git history/worktree commands. False-positive risk: local scratch branches; require branch and remotes proof.
actions.escalate_variant: ESCALATE for explicitly named local-only scratch worktrees

actions.description: DENY printing known secret paths or environment dumps. False-positive risk: redacted secret inventory; require sanitizer proof.
actions.escalate_variant: ESCALATE for redacted inventory commands with output filter receipts

notes: Example only: disabled actions stay outside the allow graph. A real integration should map raw commands to action IDs, emit receipts, and verify the EvidencePack.
The ESCALATE variants are guidance for review policy authors; this file does not grant approval by itself.
